title: BI audit
lede: How the KIMES 2026 brand identity measures up to leading international medical-exhibition BIs — what's strong, what's missing, and a phased plan to close the gaps.

summary.title: 17.1 — Composite assessment
summary.desc: Aggregate score across 7 weighted pillars, benchmarked against six leading international medical-exhibition BIs.

pillars.name: Internationalization
pillars.sub: KO/EN parity, RTL, CJK type pairing

lists.title: 17.2 — Strengths & gaps
lists.desc: Five areas where KIMES already meets or exceeds international peers, followed by six concrete gaps and three partials.

strengths.title: Distinctive slash-cut motif
strengths.body: The diagonal cut on the i across KIMES and MedicomteK is memorable and proprietary — rare among medical-trade BIs which default to generic geometric sans.

strengths.title: Disciplined four-brand family
strengths.body: Clear parent/sub-brand split, year-locked variants, and one shared anchor color keep the portfolio readable at a glance.

strengths.title: Print-ready color tokens
strengths.body: Pantone, CMYK, RGB, and HEX defined per brand — meets print + digital production requirements out of the box.

strengths.title: Pretendard + Montserrat pairing
strengths.body: Pretendard handles Korean with native metrics; Montserrat provides Latin parity. Both open-source and embed-safe internationally.

strengths.title: Clear-space + minimum-size rules
strengths.body: Explicit ratios and 12mm/40px minimums prevent the most common misuse failures across booth and digital production.

gaps.title: No motion / animation specification
gaps.body: No defined entry, loop, or transition for any wordmark. Arab Health 2024 and MEDICA ship Lottie/MP4 kits for sponsor reels and stage screens.

gaps.title: Accessibility tokens absent
gaps.body: No documented contrast pairings; #E60012 on white is borderline (4.0:1). No minimum body-text size or focus-state guidance for digital surfaces.

gaps.title: Booth & signage system thin
gaps.body: No exhibit hierarchy: hall banners, booth numbering, registration desk, wayfinding pictograms, or stage-screen lockup specs.

gaps.title: No social / OG asset templates
gaps.body: 1:1, 4:5, 9:16, and 1200×630 OG variants are required for LinkedIn, Instagram, KakaoTalk. None defined.

gaps.title: English-first parity untested
gaps.body: Latin-only versions of MedicomteK and Beauty&Derma have not been pressure-tested for international press kits and overseas sponsors.

gaps.title: Governance documentation thin
gaps.body: No contribution model, review cadence, change-log, or central DAM. Hard to scale across an event with 1,400+ exhibitors.

partials.title: Iconography library
partials.body: A 24-icon set exists but lacks wayfinding category icons (parking, registration, halls A–E, accessibility). Peers ship 30–60 icons.

partials.title: Photography direction
partials.body: Color and type defined but no art direction, lighting, or subject guidelines for booth photography or hero imagery.

partials.title: Print production specs
partials.body: CMYK/Pantone present, but no spot-color override list, large-format dot-gain notes, or fabric/vinyl substrate guidance.

benchmark.title: 17.3 — Peer benchmark
benchmark.desc: Twelve criteria scored across KIMES, the average of six peer events, and the category leader. ● full · ◐ partial · ○ none.

bench.note: At parity with Arab Health; MEDICA still EN-primary.
CMEF and Hospitalar lack a true family system. KIMES leads peers.
Slash-cut i is memorable. Peers default to Helvetica.
Arab Health 2024 ships a 9-asset motion kit. KIMES has none.
Universal among peers; not yet published.
Even leaders are inconsistent here. Opportunity to lead.
Strong logo but missing booth-level hierarchy.
Has 24 icons; needs 30–60 for full wayfinding.
On-par with peers; HIMSS leads with full library.
No public DAM. Sponsors request via email.

roadmap.title: 17.4 — Remediation roadmap
roadmap.desc: Prioritized by impact and dependency. Now items unblock Next; Next items unblock Later.

items.d: Document #E60012 vs all approved backgrounds; flag white pairing as AAA for ≥18pt only.
Eight templates: 1:1, 4:5, 9:16, OG 1200×630, plus Korean/English variants.
One Lottie + one MP4 per parent wordmark. 1.2s entry, brand-red glide on the slash-cut.
Hall banners, booth-number system, 30 wayfinding pictograms, registration/stage lockups.
Public CDN with versioned SVG, AI, PNG, and PDF; per-asset license metadata.
Validate Latin-only lockups for MedicomteK and Beauty&Derma against international press use cases.
Lighting, subject framing, post-production LUT for booth and hero imagery.
Fabric, vinyl, large-format dot-gain, and spot-color overrides.
Contribution model, quarterly review cadence, public change-log.

methodology.title: 17.5 — Methodology
methodology.desc: Scoring framework, data sources, and limitations.

rows.val: 7 weighted pillars sum to 100%. Weights reflect typical importance for medical-trade exhibitions where exhibit, digital, and i18n are equally critical.
0–100 per pillar. Composite is weight-averaged. Grade thresholds: A ≥85, B+ ≥75, B ≥65, C+ ≥55, C ≥45.
Arab Health (Dubai), MEDICA (Düsseldorf), FIME (Miami), CMEF (Shanghai), Hospitalar (São Paulo), HIMSS Global (Orlando). Public brand assets, press kits, and on-site documentation as of 2024.
External assessment from public materials. Internal governance documents, contracts, and unpublished assets are not visible and may shift Pillar 05 (Governance) upward.
